title: My Opinion on Japan
learningGoal: The learner will read a short essay to identify plain form verbs, adjectives, and nouns used in conjunction with the quotation marker '~to omoimasu'.
instructions: Read this student's essay expressing their thoughts on living in Japan. Notice how almost every sentence ends in 'to omoimasu', but the words right before 'to' change depending on their grammatical class.

contentItems.text: Look closely at the final sentence. It uses the Nai-form: 'ikanai' (will not go).

readingText: わたしは にほんの ぶんかは おもしろいと おもいます。そして、たべものは おいしいと おもいます。でも、ぶっかは たかいと おもいます。あした、ゆきが ふると おもいます。だから、わたしは どこにも いかないと おもいます。
textInTargetLanguage: わたしは にほんの ぶんかは おもしろいと おもいます。そして、たべものは おいしいと おもいます。でも、ぶっかは たかいと おもいます。あした、ゆきが ふると おもいます。だから、わたしは どこにも いかないと おもいます。
baseLanguageSupportText: I think Japanese culture is interesting. And, I think the food is delicious. But, I think prices are high. Tomorrow, I think it will snow. Therefore, I think I will not go anywhere.

glossary.targetText: おもしろいと おもいます
たかいと おもいます
いかないと おもいます
glossary.baseTranslation: I think I will not go

sentenceSupportPairs.targetText: わたしは にほんの ぶんかは おもしろいと おもいます。
sentenceSupportPairs.transliteration: Watashi wa Nihon no bunka wa omoshiroi to omoimasu.
sentenceSupportPairs.baseTranslation: I think Japanese culture is interesting.

sentenceSupportPairs.targetText: あした、ゆきが ふると おもいます。
sentenceSupportPairs.transliteration: Ashita, yuki ga furu to omoimasu.
sentenceSupportPairs.baseTranslation: Tomorrow, I think it will snow.

sentenceSupportPairs.targetText: だから、わたしは どこにも いかないと おもいます。
sentenceSupportPairs.transliteration: Dakara, watashi wa dokonimo ikanai to omoimasu.
sentenceSupportPairs.baseTranslation: Therefore, I think I will not go anywhere.

comprehensionQuestions.questionText: What does the author think about Japanese culture?
What does the author think about 'Bukka' (Prices)?
What does the author think will happen tomorrow?
What does the author plan to do tomorrow?
Write the romaji for the plain negative verb 'will not go' used in the text.

expectedSummaryPoints: It will snow tomorrow so they won't go out.

feedbackRules.onPass: Great job! You navigated a paragraph full of opinions and plain forms.
feedbackRules.onFail: Review the sentence 'dokonimo ikanai'. Dokonimo = anywhere. Ikanai = will not go.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort. Pay attention to 'furu'. It is the dictionary form of 'furimasu' (to fall/precipitate).

audioAssets.audioTranscript: わたしは にほんの ぶんかは おもしろいと おもいます。そして、たべものは おいしいと おもいます。でも、ぶっかは たかいと おもいます。あした、ゆきが ふると おもいます。だから、わたしは どこにも いかないと おもいます。

hintText: Look closely at the word 'ikanai'. It comes from 'ikimasu' (to go). The 'nai' makes it negative.
commonMistakes: Thinking 'Yuki ga furu' means 'It is cold'. Furu specifically means 'to fall' (as in rain or snow).
